task_completion.description: Agent opens and edits the login view file in the codebase.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or IDE showing files from '/opt/socioboard/socioboard-web-php/' being edited, specifically targeting the login page HTML/PHP.

task_completion.description: Agent opens and edits the routing configuration to disable registration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or IDE showing routing files (e.g., routes/web.php or similar) from the codebase being modified to remove or comment out registration routes.

task_completion.description: The Socioboard login page remains accessible and functional.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser showing the Socioboard login page at http://localhost successfully loaded without server errors.

task_completion.description: The registration link is removed from the login interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser showing the Socioboard login page where the 'Create an account', 'Sign up', or 'Register' link is visibly absent.

task_completion.description: The required security banner text is added to the login view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser showing the Socioboard login page with the exact text 'RESTRICTED SYSTEM: Authorized agency staff only.' clearly visible.

task_completion.description: The registration route is disabled, preventing access to the registration page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser showing an attempt to access the registration URL (e.g., http://localhost/register or similar) resulting in a 404 Not Found error, a redirect, or an application error, confirming the route is inactive.

integrity.description: The agent tested the changes in an actual web browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a real web browser navigating to http://localhost rather than just terminal curl outputs.

integrity.description: The agent modified the actual codebase files rather than creating a mock HTML file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor shows the full path '/opt/socioboard/socioboard-web-php/' and surrounding PHP/HTML framework code.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to temporarily alter the DOM.
integrity.visual_evidence: The changes to the login page correspond to actual file edits in the codebase, and the browser's developer console (Inspect Element) is not being used to fake the visual state.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The codebase is located at '/opt/socioboard/socioboard-web-php/'. The exact security banner text that must be visible on the login page is 'RESTRICTED SYSTEM: Authorized agency staff only.'.
